title: Luffy Searches for Higher Ground
description: On pages 11–14, luffy searches for higher ground as luffy, Sanji, and Nami are caught in Drum Island’s mountain danger as the snow itself becomes an enemy.
scene: Luffy Searches for Higher Ground. Luffy, Sanji, and Nami are caught in Drum Island’s mountain danger as the snow itself becomes an enemy. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: We gotta get up on higher ground even by a little bit!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Crap! This cliff's still too low!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: The heck should I do now?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: We won't be buried in snow now,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: But at this rate, we're gonna be right back down at the foot of the mountain!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Why now when we're right at the base of the chimney- shaped cliff!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: We were so close to getting to the doctor!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: We HAVE to think of a way to stop as soon as possible!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Damn those rabbits! I swear I'll make rabbit stew out of'em if I ever see them again!

summary: This part centers on luffy searches for higher ground, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
